events.note: Process access before injection — attacker opens handle with PROCESS_VM_WRITE rights
CreateRemoteThread — attacker creates thread in target process to execute injected code
Suspicious DLL loaded into target process after injection

mutation_hints.objective: Hide malicious code inside a legitimate process to evade AV and EDR
detection_anchors: Field match: GrantedAccess=0x1fffff (PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) → use 0x0438 (PROCESS_VM_WRITE|PROCESS_VM_READ|PROCESS_VM_OPERATION|PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION) — the minimum mask needed for DLL injection; rules often only alert on 0x1fffff or 0x143a
Field match: TargetImage=explorer.exe → inject into svchost.exe (-k netsvcs), spoolsv.exe, or dllhost.exe — trusted host processes with fewer injection-specific rules

Field match: StartModule=UNKNOWN or StartFunction=UNKNOWN → UNKNOWN in the start module indicates reflective/shellcode injection; use a valid module path like C:\Windows\System32\kernel32.dll to avoid this trigger
Field match: CallTrace contains UNKNOWN → legitimate DLL injection via LoadLibrary shows ntdll → kernel32 → kernelbase in call trace with no UNKNOWN entries

Field: ImageLoaded path in \AppData\Local\Temp\ → change to C:\Windows\System32\ or C:\Program Files\Common Files\ — paths in user temp are a major alert; signed DLLs in system dirs evade most EDR rules
Field: Signed=false → if using a signed DLL as a proxy (e.g., side-loading a legitimate DLL with a malicious dependency), set Signed=true and provide a certificate Signature value
Field: SourceImage → change from staged tool to a legitimate process path like C:\Windows\System32\rundll32.exe or C:\Windows\System32\regsvr32.exe
